title: rubus constrictus
intro: Rubus constrictus, also known as the Chinese raspberry or Chinese wineberry, is a type of edible bramble plant native to China. It is a perennial vine with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, offering several health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. Rubus constrictus is often prepared as a tea, wine, or tincture for its medicinal properties, and its fruits can be eaten fresh or used to make jams, jellies, and preserves.
